label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage. The question asks, "According to the passage, when they are looking for a missing child, they..." and gives four options. The passage says, "Of course, with nearly two million missing children every year, we can't do all these things for everyone. We haven't got the time, or the money, or the people who work for it." This directly relates to the options given. Let's go through each option: (A) can always find him - This is not supported by the passage. The passage doesn't say they can always find missing children. (B) couldn't always try every way mentioned above - The passage says, "we can't do all these things for everyone," which means they can't always use every method mentioned. This matches option B. (C) just try one way or another - The passage lists multiple methods they use (APB, calling hospitals, contacting family, using posters, etc.), so it's not just one way. This doesn't match the passage. (D) won't give up until they find him - The passage doesn't mention
